title: Zea tunicata
taxonomy: Zea tunicata is a species of wild maize or tosinte. It is a type of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. Zea tunicata is closely related to other species of Zea and is often considered a variant or subspecies. As a member of the genus Zea, it is classified as a monocot, sharing characteristics with other plants in this group. The classification of Zea tunicata is still being researched and studied by botanists.

Zea mays ssp. parvifolia, commonly known as Wild corn or Zea tunicata, is a wild grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Zea mays ssp. parvifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Zea mays ssp. parvifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
